etymology.text: According to Arthur Jeffery it appears first in discussions of dietary laws and thus is likely borrowed from Hebrew בְּהֵמָה (bəhēmā́). Else it belongs to the root ب ه م (b h m) to which there is also بَهْمَة (bahma, “lamb”). Compare Ugaritic 𐎁𐎅𐎎𐎚 (bhmt) and Ge'ez ብህመ (bəhmä, “to be dumb, to be speechless”).
